investigation.title: High error rate and latency spike on payment-service
investigation.description: Payment service began returning 15% HTTP 500 errors and p99 latency exceeded 5s at 14:32 UTC. Downstream impact on checkout and order-service observed. No deployment in preceding 4 hours.

properties.normalized_summary: HikariCP connection pool at 100% utilization. 847 threads waiting for connection. Pool size: 20, timeout: 30000ms. Errors: HikariPool-1 - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30001ms.
RDS CPUUtilization averaged 98.4% over the 14:25-14:45 UTC window. Maximum: 99.8%. Alarm threshold (80%) breached at 14:28 UTC. Concurrent connections: 487 (max: 500).

RDS slow query log shows 2,841 queries exceeding 5s threshold between 14:25-14:40 UTC. Top query: SELECT ... FROM payments JOIN payment_methods WHERE status = 'pending' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 100 - avg 12.4s. Table: payments row count increased from 8M to 47M since last VACUUM.

nodes.label: Missing index causing full table scan on payments table

properties.reasoning: Slow query log shows full sequential scan on the payments table (47M rows) for status-filtered queries. No index on (status, created_at). Connection pool exhaustion is a downstream effect of long-running queries blocking threads.

nodes.label: Table bloat due to unvacuumed dead tuples

properties.reasoning: Payments table grew from 8M to 47M rows in slow query logs, suggesting table bloat from dead tuples. VACUUM not run since last deployment 3 weeks ago. Contributing factor to query slowness.

properties.command: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_payments_status_created ON payments(status, created_at) WHERE status IN ('pending', 'processing');

evidence.normalized_summary: HikariCP connection pool at 100% utilization. 847 threads waiting for connection. Pool size: 20, timeout: 30000ms.
RDS CPUUtilization averaged 98.4% over 14:25-14:45 UTC. Concurrent connections: 487/500.
2,841 slow queries exceeding 5s. Full table scan on payments (47M rows). Missing index on status+created_at columns.

hypotheses.title: Missing index causing full table scan on payments table
hypotheses.description: The payments table (47M rows) is missing a composite index on (status, created_at). Queries filtering by status and ordering by created_at execute as sequential scans, taking 12+ seconds each. With high traffic, these long-running queries exhaust the HikariCP connection pool (max: 20), causing all incoming requests to queue and timeout.

hypotheses.title: Table bloat due to unvacuumed dead tuples increasing scan cost
hypotheses.description: The payments table appears to have significant dead tuple bloat. VACUUM has not been run since the last deployment 3 weeks ago. Dead tuples inflate the table size, increasing sequential scan cost and buffer cache pressure.

actions.title: Create composite index on payments(status, created_at)
actions.description: Create a partial composite index covering the most common query pattern. Uses CONCURRENTLY to avoid locking the table during index creation.
actions.command: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_payments_status_created ON payments(status, created_at) WHERE status IN ('pending', 'processing');
actions.outcome: Index created successfully in 342s. Query p99 latency returned to 180ms baseline within 3 minutes. Connection pool utilization dropped to 12%. Error rate returned to 0.1%.
